2605.04193.title: ANDRE: An Attention-based Neuro-symbolic Differentiable Rule Extractor
2605.04193.paper_summary: Inductive Logic Programming (ILP) aims to learn interpretable first-order rules from data, but existing symbolic and neuro-symbolic approaches struggle to scale to noisy and probabilistic settings. Classical ILP relies on discrete combinatorial rule search and is brittle under uncertainty, while differentiable ILP methods typically depend on predefined rule templates or inaccurate fuzzy operators that suffer from vanishing gradients or poor approximation of logical structure when reasoning over probabilistic predicate valuations. This paper proposes an Attention-based Neuro-symbolic Differentiable Rule Extractor (ANDRE), a novel ILP framework that learns first-order logic programs by optimizing over a continuous rule space with attention-based logical operators. ANDRE replaces both rule templates and logical operators with fully differentiable, attention-driven conjunction and disjunction operators that approximate logical min-max semantics, enabling accurate, stable, and interpretable reasoning over probabilistic data. By softly selecting, negating, or excluding predicates within each rule, ANDRE supports flexible rule induction while preserving symbolic structure. Extensive experiments on classical ILP benchmarks, large-scale knowledge bases, and synthetic datasets with probabilistic predicates and noisy supervision demonstrate that ANDRE achieves competitive or superior predictive performance while reliably recovering correct symbolic rules under uncertainty. In particular, ANDRE remains robust to moderate label noise, substantially outperforming existing differentiable ILP methods in both rule extraction quality and stability.
2605.04193.paper_summary_zh: 歸納邏輯程式設計（ILP）旨在從數據中學習可解釋的一階規則，但現有的符號和神經符號方法在噪聲和概率環境中難以擴展。經典的ILP依賴於離散的組合規則搜索，在不確定性下顯得脆弱，而可微分的ILP方法通常依賴於預定義的規則模板或不準確的模糊運算符，這些運算符在對概率謂詞評估進行推理時會遭遇梯度消失或邏輯結構近似不良的問題。本文提出了一種基於注意力的神經符號可微分規則提取器（ANDRE），這是一個新穎的ILP框架，通過在連續規則空間中優化基於注意力的邏輯運算符來學習一階邏輯程式。ANDRE用完全可微分的、基於注意力的合取和析取運算符替代了規則模板和邏輯運算符，這些運算符近似邏輯的最小-最大語義，從而實現對概率數據的準確、穩定和可解釋的推理。通過在每個規則中柔性地選擇、否定或排除謂詞，ANDRE支持靈活的規則歸納，同時保留符號結構。在經典ILP基準、大規模知識庫以及帶有概率謂詞和噪聲監督的合成數據集上進行的大量實驗表明，ANDRE在不確定性下可靠地恢復正確的符號規則，同時實現了具有競爭力或優越的預測性能。特別是，ANDRE對中等標籤噪聲保持穩健，在規則提取質量和穩定性方面大幅超越現有的可微分ILP方法。
2605.04193.author: Iman Sharifi et.al.
2605.04193.authors: Iman Sharifi, Peng Wei, Saber Fallah
